join_order.notes: custkeys are dense 1..1500000, bitset ~187KB fits in L1 cache
use lineitem_orderkey_index for contiguous range lookups into lineitem

data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map(pre_sized=2000000, key=int32_t(l_orderkey), value=struct{double revenue, int32_t o_orderdate, int32_t o_shippriority})

scan_orders.notes: each thread scans a chunk of orders, builds local aggregation map

parallelism.merge: merge thread-local hash maps after parallel phase

indexes.purpose: map custkey to row_index for mktsegment check during bitset build
contiguous range lookup for lineitem rows per qualifying orderkey

steps.name: build_customer_bitset
steps.description: Load c_mktsegment_dict.bin to find BUILDING code. Scan c_mktsegment.bin, set bit in bitset for c_custkey[i] where mktsegment matches. Bitset indexed by custkey value (dense, max ~1500000).

steps.name: scan_filter_orders_and_probe_lineitem
steps.description: Parallel morsel-driven scan of orders. For each order row: check o_orderdate[i] < 9204, then check customer_bitset[o_custkey[i]]. For qualifying orders (~1.53M), use lineitem_orderkey_index to get contiguous lineitem range. For each lineitem row in range: check l_shipdate[j] > 9204, if pass: accumulate l_extendedprice[j] * (1 - l_discount[j]) into thread-local hash map keyed by l_orderkey (= o_orderkey). Store o_orderdate and o_shippriority with the group.

steps.name: merge_and_topk
steps.description: Merge thread-local hash maps. Then single-pass top-10 selection using a min-heap of size 10, ordered by (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC).
